title: How to check if a cell contains specific text
task: Return Yes/No (or anything else) depending on whether a cell contains a word or substring.

excel.note: SEARCH is case-insensitive; use FIND for case-sensitive matching.

google_sheets.note: Identical; REGEXMATCH(A2,"red") is a Sheets-only shortcut.

explanation: SEARCH returns the position of "red" inside the text (here 6) or a #VALUE! error when it's absent. ISNUMBER turns that into TRUE/FALSE, and IF maps it to your labels. You can't write =IF(A2="*red*",...) — wildcards don't work in a plain equals test, which is the usual reason this formula surprises people. To count or sum by contains instead, COUNTIF/SUMIF do accept wildcards: =COUNTIF(A:A,"*red*").
